summary.intent: Address Codex review feedback on a PR (#682) for meter/stat/progress components in the ars-ui Rust component library, fixing bugs and responding to reviewer comments.
summary.outcome: Fixed six issues from the first Codex review round (committed as 4c1fdb33), then handled a second Codex review round by patching a valid progress i18n localization bug (committed separately) and preparing spec-based pushback for two meter/native-host comments that conflicted with the explicit implementation contract.

repo: The repo uses a Codex bot reviewer triggered via '@codex review' PR comments, with review threads that must be replied to and resolved via GitHub GraphQL API
The 'snapshot-reviewed' label must be re-applied after each push to keep the snapshot check green
CI pipeline order: Formatting → Snapshot Change Check → Clippy → Workspace Check → Mutual Exclusion Guard → Unit Tests
Clippy enforces that message callback types be exposed as public type aliases with docs, not just used inline
The spec lives in spec/components/data-display/progress.md and is validated via 'cargo xtask spec validate'
Progress and Meter components are in crates/ars-components/src/data_display/ and tests are in crates/ars-components/tests/data_display_meter_stat_progress.rs
meter role='meter' and ARIA range attributes are required by the agnostic API contract — reviewer suggestions to remove them must be pushed back with spec reference

code.finding: Progress determinate value_text must come from a localization Messages callback (i18n feature); missing this caused a regression caught by Codex
Test 'progress_determinate_value_text_comes_from_messages' validates that progress value text is sourced from the Messages provider under the i18n feature flag
Spec updated to document the Messages/value_text contract for determinate progress, used to justify pushback against Codex meter/native-host comments

workflow: Batch GitHub API calls (reply + resolve + label + trigger) in a single shell command chain to reduce round trips
Poll CI and Codex state with 'sleep N; gh pr checks; gh api graphql ...' loops rather than webhooks
Run 'cargo fmt --all --check', 'cargo clippy', and focused test suite before every commit to catch issues pre-push
Distinguish valid reviewer feedback (fix it) from out-of-scope/spec-conflicting feedback (push back with contract reference) before acting

friction: Clippy required refactoring the determinate message callback into a public type alias with docs — not anticipated, added an extra fix/rerun cycle
Codex opened new threads mid-CI-wait, requiring triage of which comments were valid vs. conflicting with the explicit implementation contract
CI jobs run sequentially in stages, requiring multiple polling sleep cycles (90s, 150s, 150s, 150s, 180s) to observe full green status
Two Codex meter comments directly contradicted the agreed spec (role='meter', ARIA range attrs), requiring spec-based pushback rather than a code fix
open_items: Reply to the two meter/native-host Codex threads with explicit spec/contract pushback and await Codex acknowledgment or further discussion
Confirm Unit Tests CI job reaches green after the i18n localization commit
Await the next '@codex review' pass to see if the two disputed meter threads are resolved or escalated
